programmes.show_description: Scott comes in with a terrible tattoo that's taken his love for Norwich City FC too far. Jay has space-loving Dan's back with a cover that's out of this world.
Greg Davies and Alex Horne pit winners Josh Widdicombe, Katherine Ryan, Rob Beckett, Noel Fielding and Bob Mortimer against each other in the ultimate test. (Ep1/2)
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson take on guest captain Richard Ayoade and Aisling Bea. Tom Allen joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
John Wilson, the president and chief operating officer of Rollins Incorporated - the parent company of eight pest control firms - goes incognito to work among his employees.
Randy DeWitt, the CEO of sports bar and grill chain Twin Peaks, goes incognito among his own staff, to find out if the company's culture is keeping up with its rapid growth.
In Colchester, touchy-feely Yvonne and Neil take on fitness fanatics Darren and Amy, and social worker Tony, who makes less-than-complimentary comments about his partner Victoria...
In east London, there's pressure in the kitchen, glamorous cuisine and a surprise performance, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
...Selling: Neither Madan nor Simone are willing to budge on their wish list. Can Drew break the deadlock? Jonathan wields power tools as he destroys dated décor.
The One with All the Pixies: Sam has £13k to spend on his pixie wedding to his love, Luanne. Will Luanne, who dreams of a traditional service, appreciate his scheme?
The One with the Dinosaurs: Elle has her heart set on a classy wedding at Liverpool's iconic Liver Building. But Phil plans a big day to go down in history.
Shane Evans, the co-founder and chief operating officer of Massage Heights, one of the biggest massage therapy chains in North America, goes undercover in her own company.
Bruce 'Two Dogs' Bozsum, chairman of the board of a Native American gaming and entertainment empire, goes undercover to ensure that the company's casinos are thriving.
Angler's Lodge: Gordon Ramsay visits Angler's Lodge in Idaho, a stunning venue whose owners have been struck by family tragedy. Can he rescue the broken business?
Vienna Inn: Gordon checks in to the Vienna Inn, an Austrian-themed hotel and restaurant in Massachusetts, where business isn't what it used to be. Can he keep their doors open?
...and Selling: Brand new series: Despite working seven days a week, city living is breaking the bank for Joe and Lana. Can the bros turn their property from rags to riches?
Out, Damned Spot: Captain Glenn doesn't let anyone off the hook when the crew get a bit rowdy during their night off. And a familiar face rejoins the crew...
Five comics create masks that look and taste great, and clear up a mess of their own making, in the showdown to end all showdowns. But who will claim the crown? (Ep2/2)
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and David Mitchell take on Jon Richardson and Sara Pascoe. Ellie White and Natasia Demetriou join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
A chance to watch top moments from the comedy game-show mash-up, with guests including Richard Ayoade, Aisling Bea, Alan Carr, David Mitchell, Katherine Ryan and Johnny Vegas.
On the Pull: Mark and Jeremy go on the pull and Mark finds himself smoking pot in the Lazerbowl toilets with a teenage goth...
Mark Makes a Friend: Mark makes a friend at a conference - but will it turn into more than just friendship? And Jeremy is forced to consider Super Hans as a replacement flatmate.
Alice gets her claws into dog-lover William's cover after he betrayed his furry friends in ink. Sketch helps Jules, who is branded with the palm-print of her ex.
Five comics create masks that look and taste great, and clear up a mess of their own making, in the showdown to end all showdowns. But who will claim the crown? (Ep2/2)
A chance to watch top moments from the comedy game-show mash-up, with guests including Richard Ayoade, Aisling Bea, Alan Carr, David Mitchell, Katherine Ryan and Johnny Vegas.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Steven Newman, CEO of Loehmann's department stores, goes undercover on the front line of his company, to ensure the outlets are implementing his plans to reinvigorate the brand.
Jane Grote Abell, the chairwoman of Donatos Pizza and daughter of the company's founder, goes undercover to ensure that her father's values are still being implemented.
Essex and east London. Spencer and Sara serve a simple Jewish feast. Lauren and Jack's menu reflects their South African and Burmese heritage. Paul and Ed's food challenges a picky palate.
There are silly dances, jokes and accents, plus plenty of pranks, as couples put their hosting skills to the test in Hertfordshire, in a week featuring Asian, Anglo-Indian and Irish food.
...Selling: Jonie was delighted when her daughter Taisha moved back in. But now there's a struggle for space. To sell their home, Jonathan and Drew must make it look brand new.
The One with Nessie: Calvin the over-enthusiastic groom plans a monster Scottish wedding at Loch Ness for him and his bride-to-be, Leanne.
The Day of the Dead One: Fiery bride Jenna is in for a scary surprise as her fiancé Nathan is planning a Mexican Day of the Dead themed wedding for her.
Joe DiDomizio, the president and CEO of a travel retailer that operates news stands, bookshops and cafés in transport terminals, goes incognito among his own employees.
Greg Miller, CEO of the Larry H. Miller Group, and co-owner of National Basketball Association team the Utah Jazz, dons a disguise to go undercover among his staff.
Town's Inn (Part 1/2): Gordon's at Harpers Ferry in West Virginia to visit The Town's Inn, a historic hotel that's so full of clutter it can barely accommodate guests.
Town's Inn (Part 2/2): Gordon continues his battle to help turn around The Town's Inn in West Virginia. Can he win over the stubborn owner and give the hotel a chance of success?
...and Selling: Brand new series: Nick and Julie are looking for a spacious home to accommodate every generation. But first, can the bros mellow the yellow in their tiny place?
All Work and No Rosé: A primary charter guest gets an unpleasant surprise that threatens to ruin the entire trip. Danni's sent on a difficult mission.
Charlotte Ritchie, Jamali Maddix, Lee Mack, Mike Wozniak and Sarah Kendall catch rats and have a smashing time delivering crockery on a scooter.
Katherine Ryan hosts a special episode celebrating 100 years of women's suffrage. Roisin Conaty and Jessica Hynes take on Sara Pascoe and Lolly Adefope. With Morgana Robinson.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Michelle Wolf take on Jon Richardson and Rob Beckett. Spencer Jones joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Rachel Riley looks after the numbers.
Dream Job: Jeremy tries to wow Toni with his new job in a music studio, while Mark gets passed over for promotion in favour of Sophie, and goes slowly round the twist.
Funeral: Death and terminal illness prove to be the keys to romantic success for Mark and Jeremy...
